props.message:
Brook Family Holding

Kin keep the gates. Kin keep the kin.

Mind the lambwisps; they wander.

NOTICE BOARD

- Lirien blesses the Source; we tend its margins.
- Dust the lantern oil weekly. Keep the wicks trimmed.
- The west road runs to the plain. The Hoarder has not reached us.
- Lambwisp count: short by three. If found, return to the paddocks.

North Paddock

Grazing — root-greens for the lambwisps.
Do not climb the stones.

East Paddock

For the older lambwisps. They listen for the bell at sundown.

South Paddock

Lambs of the season. Quiet voices, please.
Lirien's Lambwisps may not pass these stones — bless the stones, bless the kin.

props.open_message: A handful of river pebbles. Worn smooth by the brook.
Sling-stones, hoarded by the children. Eight smooth pebbles.
A heart-pulse, kept safe behind the lambwisp pen.
A small iron key. Cool against your palm.

root.speaker: Farmer Brook
root.text: Bah! Three lambwisps gone in a fortnight. They drift off chasing their own glimmers and never come back. You wouldn't be one of those wandering sorts, would you?

choices.label: I have your charm in hand.

offer.speaker: Farmer Brook
offer.text: Take this — a lambwisp charm. They follow the chime of it. If you find one drifting, give it a shake and walk it home.

post_offer.speaker: Farmer Brook
post_offer.text: Mind the brook stones near the south paddock. Slippery as a fish in butter.

have_charm.speaker: Farmer Brook
have_charm.text: Aye, that's the one. Ring it true and the wisps will come. Bless the kin, traveler.

bye.speaker: Farmer Brook
bye.text: Mind the lambwisps. Mind the kin.

root.speaker: Matriarch Brook
root.text: Well, well. A traveler at our gate. The kin keep the kettle warm — sit a moment, if you've news. Or even if you haven't.

choices.label: I have a letter from Tova.

letter.speaker: Matriarch Brook
letter.text:
From Tova? Truly?! *unfolds it, reads silently — eyes wet*
You good soul. You stubborn, lovely soul. She lives — she lives. Take this — eight pebbles for a sling-arm who carried what mattered.

letter_post.speaker: Matriarch Brook
letter_post.text: May the green hold you on every road, traveler. Tell her, if you pass her again, that the gate is always open.

after.speaker: Matriarch Brook
after.text: Bless you again for that letter, wanderer. The kettle is yours whenever you pass.

holding.speaker: Matriarch Brook
holding.text: We tend the lambwisps and we tend the gate. The brookwater runs sweet here; we keep our share and let the rest go. The Sigil of Sharing is in the very ground.

bye.speaker: Matriarch Brook
bye.text: Safe roads. The kin keep watch.

root.speaker: Barn-keeper Hod
root.text: Mind the gate, mind the latch, mind the little ones in the paddock. Lambwisps don't know fences. They just bump into them and look surprised.

wisps.speaker: Barn-keeper Hod
wisps.text: Little drifting things. Half wool, half wisp. They eat root-greens and chime when they're happy. Lose 'em to the wind if you don't ring the charm at sundown.

padd.speaker: Barn-keeper Hod
padd.text: North paddock for grazing, east for the older flock, south for the lambs. The east gate sticks. I keep meaning to plane it.

bye.speaker: Barn-keeper Hod
bye.text: Mind the latch on your way.

root.speaker: Little Wren
root.text:
*chases an invisible thing*
Come BACK, Tuffins! Tuffins is my favorite lambwisp and she is REALLY FAST and also INVISIBLE because she is hiding which is RUDE.

two.speaker: Little Wren
two.text: Have you seen her? She has — well, she has wool. And a chime. And a tiny attitude. ...You haven't, have you. Sigh. *runs off*

props.open_message: Where the brook empties into the lambwisp pen — a piece of heart, washed clean and waiting on a stone.

root.speaker: Old Stone
root.text: *A faint hum, like wind through stone…*

hint_pre.speaker: Old Stone
hint_pre.text: Some kin hum the world open. Listen for a wisp; she carries the first note.

hint_post.speaker: Old Stone
hint_post.text: You have learned to hum. Use the air at every owl-shrine.
